title: hieracium caesium subsp. opacum
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. opacum, commonly known as the lesser hawkbit, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and western Asia. It is used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving gut health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Hieracium caesium subsp. opacum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
